chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Present Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Adnan-ul-Karim Memon. C.P. No.D-3037 of 2015 Saifur Rehman....................Petitioner Vs. Province of Sindh through Secretary Irrigation & others...................Respondents Date of Decision: 05.03.2019. Mr. Atta-ur-Rehman, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Allah Bachayo Soomro, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Mr. Abdul Qadir Memon, Executive Engineer Central Sindh Mechanical Division, Jamshoro, Sindh. *********** O R D E R ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON-J: - Through the instant petition the petitioner has prayed for issuance of direction to the respondents to issue his salaries and continue his job. 2. Brief facts of the case are that petitioner was appointed as Greeser BPS-02 vide offer order dated 21.01.2013 in the office of Executive Engineer, Central Sindh Mechanical Division Jamshoro; that he joined duties on 21.01.2013 and since then he is working on the said post but respondents No.2 & 3 are not paying his salaries. Petitioner being aggrieved by and dissatisfied with the aforesaid actions of the respondents has filed this petition on 18.12.2015. 3. Mr. Atta-ur-Rehman, Learned counsel for the petitioner contends that petitioner is in regular service as Greeser BPS-02 in Central Mechanical 2 C.P. No.D-3037 of 2015 Division, Irrigation Department Jamshor but he is not being paid salaries, however, no any notice or termination letter has been issued to him by the respondents. Lastly he prayed for allowing of this petition. 4. Mr. Abdul Qadir Memon, Executive Engineer Central Sindh Mechanical Division, Jamshoro, Sindh who is present in person submits that petitioner is absent from the office since long without any intimation, however, they are ready to issue his salaries, if the petitioner joins the office. 5.

Mr. Abdul Qadir Memon, Executive Engineer Central Sindh Mechanical Division, Jamshoro, Sindh who is present in person submits that petitioner is absent from the office since long without any intimation, however, they are ready to issue his salaries, if the petitioner joins the office. 5. In view of above statement, this petition stands disposed of with direction to petitioner to join the office within 07 days thereafter, the respondents shall issue the salary of petitioner from the date of his attendance in the office. JUDGE JUDGE Irfan Ali
